content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB containing MMM non-negative integers, we define f(B)f(B)f(B) to be the number of distinct values among its prefix bitwise ORs.

Formally, let PPP be an array of length MMM, such that:


P1=B1P_1 = B_1P1​=B1​
Pi=Bi ∣ Pi−1P_i = B_i \ \mid\ P_{i-1}Pi​=Bi​ ∣ Pi−1​ for each 1<i≤M1 \lt i \leq M1<i≤M.

Here ∣\mid∣ denotes the bitwise OR operation.
f(B)f(B)f(B) is then defined to be the number of distinct elements in PPP.

You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers, satisfying 0≤Ai≤N0 \leq A_i \leq N0≤Ai​≤N.

Find the maximum possible value of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all rearrangements of AAA.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the size of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer — the maximum value of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all rearrangements of AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2×1052 \times 10^52×105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Let's look at all rearrangements of AAA.

[3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1]: the prefix OR array is [3,3,3][3, 3, 3][3,3,3], with one distinct element.
[3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2]: the prefix OR array is [3,3,3][3, 3, 3][3,3,3], with one distinct element.
[1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3]: the prefix OR array is [1,3,3][1, 3, 3][1,3,3], with two distinct elements.
[1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2]: the prefix OR array is [1,3,3][1, 3, 3][1,3,3], with two distinct elements.
[2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3]: the prefix OR array is [2,3,3][2, 3, 3][2,3,3], with two distinct elements.
[2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1]: the prefix OR array is [2,3,3][2, 3, 3][2,3,3], with two distinct elements.

The maximum number of distinct prefix ORs across all rearrangements is hence 222.
